completion_promises.criterion_text: All seven Protocol classes are concrete and pass isinstance checks against the runtime_checkable Protocols defined in pipeline/cli_steering.py
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented seven Sqlite*/AsyncTask* classes (SqliteBuildSnapshotReader, SqliteBuildCanceller, SqliteBuildResumer, SqliteStageSkipRecorder, SqlitePauseRejectResolver, AsyncTaskCanceller, AsyncTaskUpdater) in src/forge/lifecycle/persistence.py. Each class exposes the methods declared on its corresponding runtime_checkable Protocol from forge.pipeline.cli_steering. Verified via TestProtocolImplementations (7 tests, each calling isinstance against the Protocol) — all pass. Confirmed at runtime by manual REPL check showing isinstance() returns True for all seven Sqlite*/AsyncTask* classes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: apply_transition(t: Transition) -> None is the ONLY public method that issues UPDATE builds SET status = ? (verified by static analysis grep)
completion_promises.evidence: The single 'UPDATE builds SET status = ?' SQL is inside SqliteLifecyclePersistence.apply_transition. Other state-changing methods (mark_paused, SqliteBuildCanceller.mark_cancelled, SqliteBuildResumer.resume_after_skip) compose a Transition via state_machine.transition() and route it through apply_transition rather than emitting their own UPDATE. Verified by TestSingleStatusWriter.test_single_status_writer_in_src which walks src/ for the regex and asserts exactly one hit, located in lifecycle/persistence.py. Also verified by the existing test_state_machine.py grep-based static-analysis test (still passing after a small fix to add --include=*.py to skip .pyc cache).

completion_promises.criterion_text: apply_transition rejects raw kwargs — its signature accepts only Transition objects (Pydantic-validated)
completion_promises.evidence: apply_transition(self, transition: Transition) -> None has exactly one parameter named 'transition'. Body raises TypeError if isinstance(transition, Transition) is False. Verified by TestApplyTransitionSignature.test_signature_has_only_transition_param (introspects via inspect.signature) and test_apply_transition_rejects_non_transition (asserts TypeError for dict, str, None inputs).

completion_promises.criterion_text: mark_paused(build_id, request_id) writes pending_approval_request_id atomically with the state transition (single UPDATE statement)
completion_promises.evidence: mark_paused composes a Transition with pending_approval_request_id=request_id via state_machine.transition() and routes it through apply_transition. apply_transition's UPDATE statement writes both 'status = ?' and 'pending_approval_request_id = ?' in the same statement (single SQL UPDATE), guarded by BEGIN IMMEDIATE/COMMIT. Verified by TestMarkPausedAtomic.test_mark_paused_persists_request_id which checks both columns after the call.

completion_promises.criterion_text: record_pending_build(payload) writes the build row and translates IntegrityError on the UNIQUE (feature_id, correlation_id) index into a domain-level DuplicateBuildError (Group B duplicate refused)
completion_promises.evidence: record_pending_build wraps the INSERT in BEGIN IMMEDIATE / COMMIT and converts sqlite3.IntegrityError to DuplicateBuildError(feature_id, correlation_id) with ROLLBACK on the failed transaction. Verified by TestRecordPendingBuildDuplicate which seeds two builds with the same (feature_id, correlation_id), asserts DuplicateBuildError is raised, and confirms only one row exists in the table after the failed second insert.

completion_promises.criterion_text: read_status returns active builds (QUEUED, PREPARING, RUNNING, PAUSED, FINALISING) plus the last 5 terminal builds, sorted by queued_at DESC (per API-cli.md §4.2)
completion_promises.evidence: read_status issues two SELECTs (active set and terminal LIMIT 5), concatenates and sorts the result by queued_at DESC. Verified by TestReadStatus with four cases: empty db, active-only ordering, terminal cap at five (seeded 7 cancelled, asserted 5 returned), and combined active+terminal global DESC sort.

completion_promises.criterion_text: read_history(limit=50) returns at most 50 most recent builds; clamps limit to a max of e.g. 1000 to prevent unbounded queries
completion_promises.evidence: read_history clamps limit via max(0, min(limit, MAX_HISTORY_LIMIT)) where MAX_HISTORY_LIMIT=1000. Default is 50. Negative or zero limit returns []; non-int raises TypeError. Verified by TestReadHistory with four cases: default 50 (60 builds seeded → 50 returned newest-first), clamp to MAX, zero/negative, type guard.

completion_promises.criterion_text: exists_active_build(feature_id) returns True iff any build for that feature is in {QUEUED, PREPARING, RUNNING, PAUSED, FINALISING}
completion_promises.evidence: exists_active_build issues SELECT 1 ... WHERE feature_id = ? AND status IN (...) LIMIT 1 against the ACTIVE_STATES frozenset (QUEUED, PREPARING, RUNNING, PAUSED, FINALISING). Verified by TestExistsActiveBuild — empty case False, QUEUED True, three terminal states (parametrized COMPLETE/FAILED/CANCELLED) False, PAUSED True.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All write paths use BEGIN IMMEDIATE to acquire the write lock atomically (per F7 in the review report — avoids busy-retry under concurrent queue commands)
completion_promises.evidence: apply_transition, record_pending_build, and record_stage all open transactions with explicit 'BEGIN IMMEDIATE;' followed by COMMIT/ROLLBACK on the autocommit (isolation_level=None) writer connection. Verified by TestBeginImmediate.test_persistence_module_uses_begin_immediate which scans the module text for the literal 'BEGIN IMMEDIATE'. The mark_paused and Sqlite*Canceller/Resumer paths inherit this guarantee because they delegate to apply_transition.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All read paths use read_only_connect() from forge.adapters.sqlite.connect
completion_promises.evidence: SqliteLifecyclePersistence._reader() context manager opens a fresh read_only_connect() against self._db_path on every read call (read_status, read_history, read_stages, exists_active_build, find_active_or_recent). For in-memory test databases (path is empty) it transparently falls back to the writer connection — file-backed production paths always use the ro helper. Verified by TestReadPathsUseReadOnlyConnect.test_reads_against_real_file_open_a_ro_connection which monkeypatches read_only_connect with a spy and asserts every read API exercises it.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit tests cover each Protocol method against a real in-memory SQLite database
completion_promises.evidence: tests/forge/test_lifecycle_persistence.py has 60 tests across 14 TestXXX classes, every Protocol method (get_snapshot, mark_cancelled, resume_after_skip, record_skipped, record_skip_refused, resolve_as_reject, cancel_async_task, update_async_task) plus the read API (read_stages via SqliteStageLogReader) is exercised against a real SQLite database (tmp_path forge.db with apply_at_boot). 60 / 60 pass; broader 1544-test suite also passes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: No project-level lint/format runner is configured in pyproject.toml beyond pytest (no ruff/black/flake8 sections). The new module follows PEP 8 / 88-character line length / type-hinted APIs with docstrings, matching the existing forge.lifecycle.* style (state_machine.py, migrations.py, identifiers.py). Without a configured linter to run, the criterion cannot be machine-verified here; manual inspection confirms style consistency.
